questions.q: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of secondary activities?
questions.answer: b) Primary activities are completely absent in secondary activities

questions.q: Explain the difference between primary and secondary activities with two examples each.
questions.answer: Primary activities are directly related to the extraction and production of natural resources. Examples include farming and fishing. Secondary activities, on the other hand, involve the processing and manufacturing of raw materials. Examples include textile mills and food processing plants.

questions.q: What are the main features of secondary activities in an economic system?
questions.answer: The main features of secondary activities in an economic system include: (i) They are based on the processing and manufacturing of raw materials. (ii) They involve a high degree of specialization and division of labor. (iii) They require a large amount of capital investment and technological advancements. (iv) They often involve the creation of new products and services. (v) They contribute to the development of a country's infrastructure and economy.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: Secondary activities are usually characterized by a high level of ______________________ and _______________.
True or False: Secondary activities are always located in urban areas.
Describe the role of secondary activities in a country's economic development.
questions.answer: Secondary activities play a crucial role in a country's economic development by contributing to the growth of industries, creation of employment opportunities, and increase in national income. They also lead to the development of infrastructure, such as roads, railways, and ports, which facilitates the transportation of raw materials and finished goods.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a secondary activity?
Compare and contrast primary and secondary activities with respect to their location and characteristics.
questions.answer: Primary activities are often located in rural areas and are characterized by a low level of specialization and capital investment. Secondary activities, on the other hand, are usually located in urban areas and involve a high degree of specialization and capital investment.

questions.q: Explain the importance of secondary activities in the development of a country's economy.
questions.answer: Secondary activities are important in the development of a country's economy as they contribute to the growth of industries, creation of employment opportunities, and increase in national income. They also lead to the development of infrastructure, such as roads, railways, and ports, which facilitates the transportation of raw materials and finished goods.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: Secondary activities involve the processing and manufacturing of raw materials to produce _______________________ goods.
